signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry Unwind
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.29 — the pair is down 1.00% intraday as TIER-1 headlines suggest a U.S.-Iran peace memorandum is imminent, crushing oil prices by 7.05% and compressing US 10Y yields to 4.358%. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to further liquidation if Middle East tensions continue to de-escalate. While the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage structurally favors USD, the geopolitical shift triggers immediate safe-haven repatriation and carry unwinds. Watch 155.00 for a psychological break; next major catalyst is the RBNZ meeting on 27 May.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Oil Collapse
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.185% as NZD outperforms (+0.670%) while CAD lags (-0.515%) across G8 peers. The divergence is accelerated by the 7.05% collapse in WTI oil prices, which disproportionately weighs on the Loonie, while NZD is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (↑ Hike) vs BoC Hold. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs RBNZ hike bias creates a short-squeeze risk in NZD crosses if the 27 May meeting confirms a hawkish shift. NZD/CAD strength persists as long as oil remains under pressure from peace optimism.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7241 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional positioning while retail sentiment is 90% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the 5.16% monthly rally, increasing the risk of a retail capitulation squeeze if price holds above 0.7200. RBA at 4.35% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) provides a +60bp AUD carry premium, supporting the institutional long bias. Watch for retail short covering to accelerate the move toward 0.7300.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Yield Compression
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1756 — correlation vs EuroStoxx at +0.734 vs norm of -0.135 (z=+1.88σ) indicates a significant regime shift where EUR is trading as a high-beta risk asset rather than a funding currency. The 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) remains a structural headwind, but the bull flattener in the US yield curve (2s10s +40.8bp) and falling US 10Y yields are providing tactical support. Sustained upside requires EUR/USD to decouple from equity volatility or for ECB bias to shift from its current Hold stance.
